arts.ttl: Stoke City's perfect storm as Mark Robins makes plea to home stands
arts.sum: “You’ve got to put the wood in the door at some point and get that turning point and make it that and clearly at this moment in time that’s probably the biggest challenge – to try to get that consistency from having consistent players, to be able to pick a consistent team rather than chopping and changing all the time. Mark Robins, when asked about trying to find the right balance in midfield and consistency in selection, runs through what he calls ‘the perfect storm’ at Stoke City this season – but ends with an emphatic note of being at ‘a brilliant club that will turn’ as long as it can be navigated.

arts.ttl: Watford set to strike permanent deal for 24-year-old loan man
arts.sum: Watford are set to sign loan man Yasser Larouci from ESTAC Troyes on a permanent deal, according to… The post Watford set to strike permanent deal for 24-year-old loan man appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Stoke City monitoring sickness bug ahead of Watford clash
arts.sum: Mark Robins keeps injury updates close to his chest but mentioned illness a few times in a his pre-match press conference on Friday morning and he was still waiting to get a full briefing of who will be available, who will watch from the stands and who might be in their sickbed. Fitness permitting, Ben Pearson, Jordan Thompson and Bosun Lawal are options to be brought into midfield while Eric Bocat could return to defence after making his comeback from a calf injury as a substitute on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Watford host holders Manchester City in the FA Youth Cup semi-finals
arts.sum: The Under-18s, coached by Matt Bevans and Lloyd Doyley, have already claimed the scalps of two Premier League clubs to reach the last four.

arts.ttl: Stoke City call in SAS as Mark Robins looks to find psychological edge
arts.sum: Stoke are trying to inspire players to cope better with mental challenges and, if Mark Robins admits a home game in front of supporters who are desperate to get behind you is far removed from special forces operations in Bosnia or Iraq, there is still a fear factor that they need to get over to play with positivity in difficult moments. Stoke City welcomed big fan and former SAS commander Melvyn Downes to the training ground to talk to players in the build-up to a home clash with Watford (3pm).

arts.ttl: Watford boss pinpoints moment Stoke City lost 'cold night in Stoke' respect
arts.sum: The Watford manager was asked about his side proving themselves on a cold night in Stoke City - even though he will be taking his team to the Potteries on a mild Saturday afternoon - as they sit three points adrift of the Championship play-off places heading into the last 12 games of the season. The Potters' home league record between that Gray analysis and when work started to fill in the south east corner in early 2017 - and against some of the best teams on the planet in the top flight - was played 115, won 52, drew 34, lost 29, scored 160 and conceded 125.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins tackles 'false perception' on Stoke City as he acknowledges fan fury
arts.sum: We need their help as much as any other time, really, and some of them will be furious because they’re furious about the situation, coming to games and expect the team to play in a certain way but you’ve got a certain profile of player you can do only certain things with. “When that happens, confidence levels start to dip and it can look a little bit lethargic or like there is no care given, which is wrong.

arts.ttl: On This Day: Glenn Roeder, former Watford player and manager dies
arts.sum: On this day in 2021, former Watford manager and defender Glenn Roeder passed away at the age of 65 after a long battle with a brain tumour.
